claim_labels.label: COMPUTED
claim_labels.text: Five predeclared layers are frozen for the next stress packet: issuance, admissibility, sybil_finality, consensus, governance.

claim_labels.label: COMPUTED
claim_labels.text: The frozen T527 collapse map supports the preflight boundary: core collapse holds, governance conditional is True.

claim_labels.label: COMPUTED
claim_labels.text: All preflight gates pass: t549_route_authority, t527_review_only_boundary, five_layer_contract_predeclared, collapse_predictions_predeclared, t527_collapse_map_matches_contract, governance_conditional_preserved, next_packet_specific, governance_boundaries_preserved.

claim_labels.label: ARGUED
claim_labels.text: The next useful TAF11 burden is not another route router but a native stress packet asking whether the frozen stack predicts collapse and rescue without post-hoc layer choice.

claim_ledger_update: No claim-ledger update is earned. T550 is preflight routing and contract registration only; it leaves claim rows, Canon Index tiers, canon verdicts, and public posture unchanged.

gate_decisions.reason: T549 selected exactly the Observerse protocol-stack ablation preflight route.
T527 is usable only as frozen review-only source evidence.
The minimal near-term stack contract names exactly five layers.
Every layer has a source variable and a collapse-mode prediction.
Every contracted layer matches the T527 collapse-band evidence under near-term governance.
Governance stays conditional: five layers near-term, four if full-horizon rules are granted.
A single next executable stress packet is named.
No claim, canon, public-posture, TAF4, TAF8, external, or cross-repo movement is attempted.

hostile_controls.blocks: Treating T527 or T550 as Observerse validation, S1 evidence, or source-law proof.
hostile_controls.reason: T527 is illustration-grade and T550 is a preflight contract.

hostile_controls.blocks: Adding, dropping, or renaming layers after seeing stress-packet outcomes.
hostile_controls.reason: T550 freezes the source variables and collapse predictions before T551.

hostile_controls.blocks: Dropping governance unconditionally from the minimal near-term stack.
hostile_controls.reason: T527 makes governance load-bearing when fixed rules do not anticipate full novelty.

hostile_controls.blocks: Repairing APRD or reusing APRD family-local debt as the protocol-stack rule.
hostile_controls.reason: T548 narrowed APRD and T549 selected a new post-APRD route.

hostile_controls.blocks: Importing cross-repo truth, Lorentzian targets, or outcome labels into the stack.
hostile_controls.reason: The next packet must be finality-native and predeclared.

hostile_controls.blocks: Moving TAF4 or source-law status directly from the preflight.
hostile_controls.reason: Only a later stress survivor could create evidence; T550 creates none.

hostile_controls.blocks: Changing claim rows, Canon Index tiers, canon verdicts, public posture, or publication state.
hostile_controls.reason: The packet is review-only and has no governance movement authority.

layer_contracts.predeclared_signal: positive issued novelty before any coherence or consensus reading
layer_contracts.source_law_burden: No new record candidates arrive, so structure stops accumulating.

layer_contracts.predeclared_signal: predeclared rule filter separates legal from contradictory extensions
layer_contracts.source_law_burden: Contradictory extensions accumulate, so coherent structure collapses.

layer_contracts.predeclared_signal: identity/finality guard prevents repeated unfounded observers
layer_contracts.source_law_burden: Fake-observer capture erodes coherence even with issuance and rules.

layer_contracts.predeclared_signal: settlement relation decides when admitted records are common
layer_contracts.source_law_burden: Local worlds remain active but fail to become shared structure.

layer_contracts.predeclared_signal: rule horizon and update policy are declared before novelty is read
layer_contracts.source_law_burden: Fixed near-term rules reject novelty beyond their anticipation horizon.

not_claimed: T550 does not validate Observerse, establish a source law, prove a shadow-protection theorem, derive spacetime, prove manifoldlikeness, repair T528, reverse T223, unpause S1, promote S1, change claim status, change Canon Index tiers, change canon verdicts, change public posture, change the North Star, authorize external publication, move TAF4, execute TAF8, or move cross-repo truth. It is a review-only preflight contract for a later protocol-stack source-law stress packet.
recommended_next: Run t551_observerse_protocol_stack_source_law_stress_packet. It should keep these five layer contracts frozen, test whether the stack predicts collapse/rescue on a native finality fixture before target-outcome reading, and reject if it overreads T527, drops conditional governance, retunes APRD, imports cross-repo truth, or moves TAF4/source-law status directly.
taf11_update: TAF11 remains active. The post-APRD protocol-stack route is now preflighted as a five-layer, review-only contract; the next swing must stress whether that stack has source-law force on a native fixture.
taf4_update: TAF4 remains blocked. T550 supplies no finite-to-continuum bridge and turns the Observerse stack only into a later TAF11 stress packet.
taf8_update: TAF8 remains waiting for a real domain-native cross-domain packet. T550 does not execute or strengthen the T541 transfer gate.
